title: Uniface 10.3.02: Consolidated Updates since 10.3.01

Uniface 10.3.02: Consolidated Updates since 10.3.01

Uniface 10.3.02: Consolidated Updates since 10.3.01

Uniface 10.3.02 consolidates new functionality and bug fixes that have been provided in patches since 10.3.01.

IDE Usability

Feature: Smart suggestions in Main U-Bar.

It is now easier to find and open objects using the U-Bar. The behavior of the U-Bar has been changed to provide suggestions in a drop-down list as you type, instead of having to retrieve and browse through long lists of objects. Suggestions are offered in order of relevance, depending on what you are entering (an object type, object name, or other part of a URL).

Wildcards (* and ?) and Uniface profile characters (Gold+*, Gold+?) are supported.

It is also possible to restrict your search to specific component types using component alias codes instead of cpt. For example cptfrm for forms, cptdsp for dynamic server pages, cptmdl for modeled components, and so on.

By default, 50 items are returned, but you can configure the maximum number of results using the IDE logical UBAR_RESULTS_MAXHITS.

For more information, see Find and Open Objects Using the Main U-Bar, U-Bar Syntax, and Uniface IDE Logicals.

Feature: Improved navigation to objects and ProcScript modules.

In the Project Editor you can now double-click a main development object to open it. In previous releases, the only way to open an object was to choose Open from the context menu.

For more information, see Open a Main Development Object for Editing.

In component and entity editors, double-click an object in any worksheet to go directly to the script container for the object in the Write Script worksheet. For more information, see Navigate to ProcScript modules.

Feature: One-step Compile & Test for components.

You can now compile and test components in one step using the Compile & Test option. This is available in the component editor and in the context menu for components in the Project Editor. For more information, see Compilation and Compile a Project.

Feature: Improved Search & Replace functionality in the Write Script worksheet.

The Quick Search box enables to search and replace one or more occurrences of a search string in the Write Script worksheet:

For more information, see Quick Search and Find and Replace Strings in Code.

IDE Editors

Feature: The Global ProcScripts editor has been replaced with a standard IDE editor.

You can create and edit individual global ProcScript modules in the new Global ProcScript Library Editor. The classic editor for global ProcScripts has been replaced by a standard IDE editor. It is now possible to create Global ProcScript libraries in the Project Editor, add existing global ProcScript libraries to a project, and navigate directly to global ProcScript libraries in the U-Bar.

If you want to use your existing Repository and ide.asn file, you need to:

  1. Add the following logicals to the ide.asnfile:

    ; Default palettes for Global ProcScript Library Editor:
GlobalProcLibraryEditor_DefineStructure_ResourceBrowser_DefaultPalette = libprc:UPALETTE_LIBPRC
GlobalProcLibraryEditor_WriteScript_ResourceBrowser_DefaultPalette = libsnp:USCRIPT_COMMON
  2. Start the Uniface IDE and re-import utemplates.xml, which contains the new palette and templates for Global ProcScripts.

For more information, see Global ProcScripts and Creating Global ProcScripts.

Feature: The Load Definitions facility is now available for Oracle and PostgreSQL.

The Project Editor has been enhanced to support the Load Definitions facility for Oracle and PostgreSQL. This enables you to create modeled entities using the definitions of your existing Oracle or PostgreSQL database.

For more information, see Load Definitions for PostgreSQL and Load Definitions for Oracle.

Feature: IDE Customization APIs provide enhanced navigation functionality.

The IDE API and Menu callback operations now enable you to navigate to any object, worksheet, or location supported by the U-Bar syntax. The following functions have been enhanced to support the full URL syntax string (including worksheet and window options) used in the U-Bar.

  • IDE API: navigateTo
  • Menu callback operations: globalMenuCallback and editorMenuCallback

For more information, see IDE API Operations, globalMenuCallback, and editorMenuCallback.

Other Features

Feature: The TLS Connector can now be used with the WRD, SRD, and Debugger.

The TLS connector supports encrypted network connections between the Uniface Router and Uniface servlets—the Web Request Dispatcher (WRD) and SOAP Request Dispatcher (SRD).

For more information, see TLS Connections for Uniface Servlets (WRD and SRD) and Configure a TLS Connection for a Uniface Servlet (WRD or SRD) .

When debugging a Uniface application over a network, it is now possible to use TLS instead of TCP. This can be useful for remote debugging of web and mobile applications. For more information, see /deb and Debugging Remote and Server Processes.

Feature: A new API enables you to get the DOM node associated with a DSP widget.

The function uniface.Field.getBoundElement() returns the outermost DOM node of the associated DSP widget. For more information, see getBoundElement().

Feature: New option for configuring the Uniface application's connection with the DLM license server.

The $LICENSE_OPTIONS assignment setting now supports a HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL option that lets you set the frequency with which a client application notifies the DLM license server that a license is still in use. By default, this is 300 seconds (5 minutes), but it can be set to as little as 15 seconds. For more information, see $LICENSE_OPTIONS(DLM) in the Uniface Library.

Currency

Feature: The iSeries has been added as a supported platform.

For more information, see Installing and Configuring Uniface on iSeries or the iSeries Installation and Configuration Guide (PDF).

Bug Fixes

Issue 31809: Converting attributes with structToXml/schema

When converting a struct to XML using a schema, the schema must define any attributes to be converted. Otherwise they are ignored with no warning.

If the schema defines <anyAttibute> for an element, the attribute declaration must also be available in the schema. If no attribute definition is found, a warning is reported in $procreturncontextand no attributes are written to the XML.

For more information, see structToXml/schema.

Issue 31853: Runtime counters are supported in Uniface 10.

You can use the numgen and numset ProcScript statements to define and increment runtime counters, but it is not possible to define and set initial values using a counter editor. The UOBJ.TEXT entity definition is once again available in the meta dictionary umeta.xml. For more information, see Migration of Counters.

Issue 31887: It is no longer necessary to delete the state file and restart the IDE when changing or adding worksheets to an editor.

When you edit an IDE plug-in logical to add or change the name of a worksheet plug-in, the IDE detects this and restarts the affected editor.

Note: The IDE does not detect changes made to the title and tooltip in the getWorksheetOptions operation. If you change these, you need to restart your editor for the changes to take effect.

For more information, see Add User-Defined Worksheets to IDE Editors and getWorksheetOptions .

Issue 31890: New logical to enable custom worksheets in the Global ProcScript Editor.

Use the logical IDE_DEFINE_WORKSHEETS_LIBPRC to add custom worksheets to the Global ProcScript editor. For more information, see Logicals for IDE Menu and Worksheet Plug-Ins.

Issue 31903: During migration, Form components with ambiguous layout definitions are rejected.

In Uniface 9 (and earlier versions), the Form Editor allows you to create components with ambiguous definitions, in which two entities, ENT1 and ENT2, are drawn in such a way that ENT2 is logically inside ENT1, while in the component's structure, entity ENT2 occurs before entity ENT1.

In Uniface 10, such a component is rejected during migration.

For more information, see Error: Formpic conversion to Struct has detected an error.

The ulibrary.chm file can now be renamed without losing styling.

In recent releases, renaming the ulibrary.chm file resulted in the loss of styling and some images. This problem has now been corrected.

Was this topic helpful?
LikeDislike
